title: morisonia quiriguensis
intro: Morisonia quiriguensis is a plant species native to Central America, particularly in Guatemala, known for its medicinal properties and traditional uses. It is a shrub-like plant with small white flowers and is commonly referred to as "Sacred Flower" or "Quetzal Flower" due to its association with the Quetzal bird in Mayan mythology. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including fever, cough, and respiratory problems, due to its expectorant properties. Morisonia quiriguensis is often prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and poultices, and its medicinal benefits include reduced inflammation, improved respiratory health, and enhanced immune system function.
